data.title: Snap-on Inc. surpasses Q3 estimates with $1.19 billion in net sales, 3% organic sales growth, $5.02 EPS, and 26.9% operating margin.
data.description: Snap-on Inc. (NYSE: SNA) saw a rise in shares following the release of their third-quarter 2025 results, which surpassed Wall Street projections. The boost was driven by increased activity in OEM dealerships, repair shops, and critical industries, as well as solid margins and efficient global operations. Net sales climbed 3.8% to $1.19 billion, higher than the anticipated $1.16 billion. Organic sales also saw a 3% uptick. The gross margin remained steady at 50.9%. Diluted earnings per share stood at $5.02, inclusive of a $0.31 per-share benefit from a legal settlement, up from $4.70 in the previous year. Adjusted EPS came in at $4.71, surpassing the estimated $4.63. Operating earnings before financial services reached $278.5 million, including a $22 million boost from the settlement, compared to $252.4 million in the prior year. Consolidated operating earnings grew to $347.4 million from $324.1 million, indicating a 26.9% operating margin. The effective tax rate was 22.6%.

data.title: American Water brings attention to the importance of water during 11th Imagine a Day Without Water event. AWK is the largest water and wastewater utility in the U.S. Cheryl Norton advocates for water conservation and infrastructure investment.
data.description: In Camden, New Jersey, American Water (NYSE: AWK), the top regulated water and wastewater utility firm in the U.S., underscores Imagine a Day Without Water. This initiative aims to raise awareness about the significance of water conservation and sustained investment in water and wastewater infrastructure. Cheryl Norton, Ameri's EVP and Chief Operating Officer, emphasized that water is essential for robust and flourishing communities. The company plays a key role in advocating for actions that support the preservation of this vital resource.

data.title: Grayscale introduces crypto staking to Wall Street with spot ETFs, merging rewards with traditional finance.
data.description: The recently launched spot crypto ETFs by Grayscale offer mainstream investors a regulated way to earn staking yields. This unique opportunity merges crypto rewards with traditional exposure to Wall Street. The ETFs provide a new avenue for investors to participate in the crypto market, appealing to those seeking a blend of innovation and established financial practices. With the growing popularity of cryptocurrencies, these ETFs offer a way to access this market while incorporating elements of traditional finance.

data.title: Bitcoin trader advises 'lock in' as buyers enter below $110K, with smaller investors purchasing as whales pause sell-off.
data.description: The price of Bitcoin experienced a drop, retesting support levels below $110,000. Data indicated that small investors were purchasing while large holders were reducing their substantial Bitcoin sales.

data.title: BD CFO Chris DelOrefice to exit on Dec 5, interim CFO Vitor Roque named. Stock falls. Successor search ongoing.
data.description: On Wednesday, BD (Becton, Dickinson and Company) announced that Chris DelOrefice, Executive Vice President and CFO, will be leaving the company on December 5. The company is currently seeking a permanent replacement for DelOrefice. In the interim, Vitor Roque, Senior Vice President of finance, business units, and corporate financial planning and analysis, will serve as the acting CFO starting from DelOrefice's departure date. Roque will collaborate with DelOrefice and the company's leadership to ensure a seamless transition. For more details, please refer to the full article available elsewhere.

data.title: Forvis Mazars sued for sexual harassment, gender discrimination, and retaliatory termination by female exec. Federal lawsuit alleges systemic bias by global accounting giant.
data.description: A former Managing Director at Forvis Mazars has filed a federal lawsuit in New York on October 16, 2025. Corinna Creedon, a distinguished executive at Forvis Mazars LLP, has accused the global accounting giant of systemic gender bias and retaliation by senior leadership. The lawsuit highlights instances of discrimination and mistreatment experienced by Creedon during her time at the company. The former Managing Director, known for leading the National Nonprofit division, alleges that the senior management at Forvis Mazars created a hostile work environment due to her gender. The lawsuit sheds light on significant issues within the organization, with specific instances of gender discrimination and retaliation outlined in the legal proceedings. Creedon's claims suggest a pattern of biased behavior towards female employees and misconduct by the company's leadership. The lawsuit seeks to address these grievances and bring attention to the alleged injustices faced by the former executive.

data.title: Archer Aviation buys patents, stock jumps. Cathie Wood sells ARK Invest shares. News from Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: Archer Aviation, a leading air taxi manufacturer, enhances its operations by acquiring patents. This move resulted in a significant increase in ACHR stock. However, ARK Invest, led by Cathie Wood, decided to sell off some shares of the company. This development caused a surge in Archer Aviation's stock, garnering attention from investors and industry analysts.

data.title: Charles Schwab and Interactive Brokers aim to hit buy points after strong Q3 reports. Trading signals positive.
data.description: Shares of Charles Schwab were seen attempting to break through a trendline following the company's impressive performance in Q3. Meanwhile, Interactive Brokers was trading within a favorable buy zone leading up to its upcoming results. Both companies are closely watched as they approach potential buy points in light of their recent financial reports. This news was highlighted in a recent article by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: ResearchAndMarkets.com: Sweden's colocation data center analysis report for 2025 reveals upcoming capacity over 900 MW, 5.7x larger than existing. Stockholm houses 80% of current rack capacity. Leading operators include EcoDataCenter, Conapto, Digital Realty.
data.description: Dublin - A database on "Sweden Existing & Upcoming Data Center Portfolio" has been newly included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. EcoDataCenter is currently the top operator based on rack capacity, with Conapto and Digital Realty following closely behind. Notably, atNorth and EcoDataCenter are making significant investments in expansive expansions. The forthcoming capacity is reported to be exceeding 900 MW, nearly 5.7 times larger than the current capacity. Approximately 80% of the existing rack capacity is centered in Stockholm, solidifying it as the primary location for data centers in Sweden.

data.title: Legal threats issued as Fetch.ai CEO accuses Ocean Protocol of mishandling ASI tokens. Binance restricts deposits amid feud.
data.description: The CEO of Fetch.ai, Humayun Sheikh, has alleged that Ocean Protocol mishandled ASI tokens. Sheikh has vowed to support class-action lawsuits to reveal the truth behind the matter.

data.title: Industrial X Unleashed event features leading companies showcasing real-world applications of Industrial AI on Nov 13, 2025 in New York.
data.description: Leading companies such as IFS, Anthropic, Boston Dynamics, MIT CISR, Microsoft, PwC, and Siemens are set to present cutting-edge real-world applications of Industrial AI at a showcase event on November 13, 2025, in New York. This event will shed light on the future landscape of innovation and the transformative impact of AI technologies. Mark your calendars for a glimpse into the groundbreaking developments shaping industries.

data.title: Belgium's data center market shows promise with key locations, existing & upcoming floor space, & IT load capacity. Leading operators include Digital Realty, EdgeConneX, LCL, & Datacenter United. Upcoming power capacity expansions aim to enhance digital infrastructure readiness for AI, HPC, & cloud workloads. Players like KEVLINX and LC contributing to developments. Retail & wholesale pricing also analyzed.
data.description: A new database, "Belgium Existing & Upcoming Data Center Portfolio," has recently been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The data shows that the majority of the current Rack capacity is centered in Brussels. Key data center operators in Belgium include Digital Realty, EdgeConneX, LCL, and Datacenter United. The expanding power capacity expected in upcoming projects will enhance Belgium's digital infrastructure preparedness for Artificial Intelligence (AI), High-Performance Computing (HPC), and cloud workloads. Noteworthy developments are anticipated from companies such as KEVLINX and LC, aiming to further strengthen the data center market in Belgium.

data.title: Portugal's colocation data center market booming with upcoming capacity 6x larger than existing, over 50,000 racks expected. Lisbon set to lead in new developments.
data.description: Dublin - A recent addition to the ResearchAndMarkets.com database is the "Portugal Existing & Upcoming Data Center Portfolio." The upcoming data center power capacity is projected to exceed the existing capacity by more than sixfold. It is anticipated that the total upcoming rack capacity will surpass 50,000 racks. The major upcoming data center developers in Portugal are Start Campus and Merlin Properties in collaboration with Edged Energy. Lisbon is expected to be the primary location dominating the upcoming data center market in the region.
